title: Add tool-use and error event display
description: Display tool_use events as one-line indicators and error events with prefix during streaming.
design:
## Context
Extends the chat loop to handle non-text event types per spec 02 event display rules. Adds switch cases to the for-await-of event loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tool_use events print "⚙ Using [tool name]..." on a single line
- [ ] tool_result events are silently skipped
- [ ] error events print "Error: [message]" with distinct prefix
- [ ] system events are silently skipped
- [ ] Tool indicators appear inline between text chunks (not buffered)

## Files
- `examples/chat.ts` (modify) — add event type switch cases to event loop

## Patterns
- See `specs/archive/01-types-and-constants.md` for CliEvent union type
- tool_use event: event.type === 'tool_use', tool name at event.tool.name
- error event: event.type === 'error', message at event.content
- Use switch(event.type) to dispatch; default case ignores unknown events

## Tests
- Manual: ask the CLI to read a file → should see ⚙ indicator before response text
- Manual: ask CLI something that triggers multiple tools → verify each shows indicator
- Boundary: response with only tool_use events (no text) → indicators shown, prompt re-enables

## Verify
`npx tsx examples/chat.ts` — ask CLI to use a tool, verify indicator appears inline

title: Implement async event queue for CliProcess.events
description: Create the internal event queue (array + resolver pattern) that backs AsyncIterable<CliEvent>
design:
## Context
This is the foundation for CliProcess.events. It decouples event producers (parseLine) from consumers (for-await). This is a standalone data structure with no process dependencies, making it ideal as the first task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EventQueue class implements AsyncIterable<CliEvent>
- [ ] push() buffers events when consumer is not awaiting
- [ ] push() resolves immediately when consumer is waiting
- [ ] close() signals end of iteration (for-await loop exits)
- [ ] error() causes the iterator to throw the given error
- [ ] Iterator return() (early break) marks queue as abandoned but does not error

## Files
- `src/core/event-queue.ts` (create) — parent dir will be created by scaffolding task spawner-cpx

## Patterns
- Standard async iterator protocol: Symbol.asyncIterator, next(), return()
- See Node.js stream.Readable[Symbol.asyncIterator] for reference pattern

## Tests
- Push 3 events, iterate, expect all 3 in order
- Push then close, expect iteration completes
- Close empty queue, expect immediate completion
- error() causes for-await to throw
- break in for-await calls return(), subsequent push() is discarded
- Concurrent: push while consumer awaits resolves immediately

## Verify
`npx vitest run src/core/event-queue.test.ts`

title: Implement error classification module
description: Create src/core/errors.ts with the public classifyError function that delegates to adapter.classifyError and shared pattern-matching helpers
design:
## Context
Public classifyError(cli, exitCode, stderr, stdout) delegates to adapter. Shared helpers include regex pattern table and parseRetryAfterMs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] classifyError delegates to adapter.classifyError
- [ ] Pattern matching in priority order: rate_limit → auth → session_not_found → model_not_found → context_overflow → permission_denied
- [ ] All pattern matching is case-insensitive
- [ ] First match wins
- [ ] parseRetryAfterMs extracts seconds from text (regex), converts to ms, defaults to 60000
- [ ] parseRetryAfterMs handles: 'retry after 30 seconds' → 30000, no match → 60000, malformed number → 60000
- [ ] Fallback: non-zero exit → fatal, otherwise → unknown
- [ ] raw field contains full stderr + stdout concatenated
- [ ] Message extraction: use the most relevant matching line from stderr, not the full dump

## Files
- `src/core/errors.ts` (create) — parent dir src/core/ exists

## Patterns
- See specs/archive/06-error-classification.md for pattern table, parseRetryAfterMs, and message extraction

## Tests
- rate_limit stderr → { code: 'rate_limit', retryable: true, retryAfterMs: 60000 }
- auth stderr → { code: 'auth', retryable: false }
- 'retry after 30 seconds' → retryAfterMs: 30000
- malformed retry-after text → retryAfterMs: 60000 (default)
- no match + non-zero exit → fatal
- no match + zero exit → unknown
- multiline stderr with multiple patterns → first (highest priority) wins
- case variation ('Rate Limit' vs 'rate limit') → both match

`npx tsc --noEmit`

title: Create core detect module with spawn helper
description: Create src/core/detect.ts with detect(), detectAll(), and internal execCommand helper for spawning with timeout and ENOENT handling.
design:
## Context
Core orchestrator for detection. detect(cli) looks up the adapter via getAdapter() and calls adapter.detect(). detectAll() runs all three concurrently via Promise.all. Also exports an internal execCommand helper that adapters use to spawn a CLI command with a 10s timeout, returning stdout/stderr/exitCode or an error code (ENOENT, TIMEOUT).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] detect(cli) returns Promise<DetectResult> by delegating to adapter.detect()
- [ ] detectAll() runs detect for all three CLIs concurrently and returns Record<CliName, DetectResult>

## Context
This is the core orchestration function. It ties together child_process.spawn, stdin delivery, readline on stdout, the event queue, and the done promise. Depends on the event queue being available. title: Implement spawn core with interrupt and done
description: Create src/core/spawn.ts — the main spawn() function that orchestrates child_process.spawn, stdin delivery, stream wiring, interrupt/abort, done promise, and duration tracking
design:
## Context
Tracer task 5. This is the central orchestrator. It ties together command building, stream parsing, and process lifecycle. Depends on event queue, stream wiring, and at least one adapter (Claude).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] spawn() is synchronous — returns CliProcess immediately
- [ ] CliProcess has events (AsyncIterable), pid, interrupt(), done (Promise)
- [ ] Stdin: writes prompt then closes stdin (empty prompt still written and closed — behavior is CLI-specific)
- [ ] ENOENT: done rejects with binary_not_found CliError, events throws
- [ ] Non-zero exit: classifyError() populates CliResult.error
- [ ] Nonexistent cwd: done rejects with fatal CliError (message: 'working directory not found')
- [ ] interrupt(graceMs): SIGTERM → wait graceMs (default 5000) → SIGKILL sequence
- [ ] AbortSignal: triggers interrupt; listener removed on exit (no leak)
- [ ] Done event emitted as final event in iterator
- [ ] durationMs tracked from spawn to exit
- [ ] Early iterator abandonment: stop pushing events but do NOT kill the process; done still resolves
- [ ] Readline interface on stdout closed when process exits (no stream leak)
- [ ] env passed through to child_process.spawn (inherits process.env + additions)

- Events flow through iterator in order
- ENOENT rejects done with binary_not_found
- Nonexistent cwd rejects done with fatal error

- AbortSignal triggers interrupt; listener removed after exit

- [ ] Rate limit error: detect via CliResult.error.code or error event, display "Rate limited — try again"
- [ ] CLI binary disappears: spawn throws/rejects, catch and display error, prompt re-enabled
- [ ] Non-zero exit code: check CliResult.exitCode !== 0 after event loop, display CliResult.error.message
- [ ] Response with no text events (only tool use): tool indicators shown by spawner-0jv, prompt re-enables normally via done event

- See `specs/archive/01-types-and-constants.md` for CliResult.error field
- Wrap for-await-of in try/catch for stream errors
- After event loop, check: if (result.error) display error
- Wrap spawn() call in try/catch for ENOENT / spawn failures
- Always re-enable prompt in finally block

title: Handle detection timeout for individual CLIs

design:
## Context

- [ ] If one CLI detection times out, the other CLIs still appear in the list

- [ ] Selecting a timed-out CLI still proceeds to status line

- `detectAll()` uses `Promise.all` with per-CLI 10s timeout — see `src/core/detect.ts:64-72`
- `ExecResult | ExecError` union — timeout returns `{ kind: 'timeout' }` — see `src/core/detect.ts:11`
- Adapter `detect()` maps timeout to `installed: true, version: null`

- push() becomes no-op when abandoned

- Process continues running after iterator abandoned

title: Implement interrupt() with SIGTERM/SIGKILL sequence
description: Add the interrupt method to CliProcess with graceful SIGTERM → timeout → SIGKILL escalation
design:
## Context
interrupt() is part of the CliProcess returned by spawn(). It sends SIGTERM, waits graceMs (default 5000), then SIGKILL if the process hasn't exited. This completes the process lifecycle management.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] interrupt(graceMs?) sends SIGTERM first
- [ ] If process exits within graceMs, SIGKILL is not sent
- [ ] If process does not exit within graceMs, SIGKILL is sent
- [ ] Default graceMs is 5000
- [ ] interrupt() returns the done promise (resolves to CliResult)
- [ ] Calling interrupt() on an already-exited process is a no-op (returns done)
- [ ] Timer is cleared if process exits before graceMs

## Files
- `src/core/spawn.ts` (modify) — interrupt logic integrated into spawn function

## Patterns
- child.kill('SIGTERM') followed by setTimeout race
- Use Promise.race([processExit, timeout]) pattern
- Clear timeout on process exit to avoid leaks

## Tests
- interrupt() sends SIGTERM to running process
- Process that exits quickly does not receive SIGKILL
- Process that ignores SIGTERM receives SIGKILL after graceMs
- interrupt() on exited process returns done immediately
- Custom graceMs is respected
- Timer is cleaned up on normal exit
